stem: An airport has computers connected together on a Local Area Network (LAN) using wired connections.
model_answer: Two benefits, each with an expansion applied to the airport context. Benefit 1 e.g. 'Fast/high bandwidth connection' expanded with 'so check-in desks can query the passenger database without delays'. Benefit 2 e.g. 'Secure transmission, unlikely to be intercepted' expanded with 'so passenger and staff data is not exposed to people in the terminal'. Other accepted benefits: little interference / reliable (flight status arrives without delay); long range (covers a large floor area / multiple terminals). Each answer space should contain one benefit + one expansion.
review_notes: 2024 J277/01 Q2(b)(i). 4 marks total (1 mark per benefit, 1 mark per expansion applied to airport). Cost alone is NOT creditable. 'Faster to connect' on its own is NOT enough.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe two benefits to the airport of using wired connections in their network. Apply each benefit to the airport context.

mark_points.text: Benefit 1: fast connection / high bandwidth / consistent bandwidth
Benefit 1 expansion applied to airport (e.g. reduces delays at check-in / supports live flight data)
Benefit 2: secure / unlikely to be intercepted / data transmissions are safe
Benefit 2 expansion applied to airport (e.g. passenger or aircraft data is not intercepted)

misconceptions.label: Cost alone
misconceptions.description: Answers that rely only on 'cheaper' are not creditworthy because the question is about benefits, and cost is treated as context, not a benefit.

misconceptions.label: No airport context
misconceptions.description: Generic benefits without an airport-specific expansion cap the mark at 3/4.
